title: glaucium persicum
intro: Glaucium persicum, also known as the Persian poppy or Horned poppy, is a flowering plant native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow, orange, or red flowers with distinctive horn-like projections. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Glaucium persicum is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and seeds are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are used to harness its medicinal properties.
